introSection: Boomerang is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags St. Louis in Eureka, Missouri, USA. It is a Boomerang built by Vekoma, and previously operated from March 18, 1989 to September 3, 2012 at Six Flags Over Texas as Flashback.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride first opened at Six Flags Over Texas on March 18, 1989 as Flashback. It was built in the Good Times Square area on the former site of Texas Tornado, which was relocated within the park.

Flashback was temporarily closed in the spring of 1998 after an incident happened on Demon at Six Flags Great America.

The station's handrails were repainted in 2006.

On August 2, 2012, Six Flags Over Texas announced that Flashback, along with Texas Chute Out, would close permanently. Both rides ceased operations on September 3, 2012. On August 30, 2012, Six Flags St. Louis announced that they would be getting Flashback. It was repainted and renamed Boomerang. Adjustments were made to the ride structure at its new location. The ride opened at its current location on June 8, 2013.

At Six Flags Over Texas, the Flashback sign was retained and has been featured in the "Ride Graveyard" area at Halloween events.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: While at Six Flags Over Texas, the ride was painted with red track with blue supports. When it was relocated to Six Flags St. Louis, the track was repainted green while the supports were repainted orange.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: During its later years at Six Flags Over Texas, the train was ad-wrapped, advertising Charter Communications.

fullText:
Boomerang is a steel shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags St. Louis in Eureka, Missouri, USA. It is a Boomerang built by Vekoma, and previously operated from March 18, 1989 to September 3, 2012 at Six Flags Over Texas as Flashback.

The ride first opened at Six Flags Over Texas on March 18, 1989 as Flashback. It was built in the Good Times Square area on the former site of Texas Tornado, which was relocated within the park.

Flashback was temporarily closed in the spring of 1998 after an incident happened on Demon at Six Flags Great America.

The station's handrails were repainted in 2006.

On August 2, 2012, Six Flags Over Texas announced that Flashback, along with Texas Chute Out, would close permanently. Both rides ceased operations on September 3, 2012. On August 30, 2012, Six Flags St. Louis announced that they would be getting Flashback. It was repainted and renamed Boomerang. Adjustments were made to the ride structure at its new location. The ride opened at its current location on June 8, 2013.

At Six Flags Over Texas, the Flashback sign was retained and has been featured in the "Ride Graveyard" area at Halloween events.



While at Six Flags Over Texas, the ride was painted with red track with blue supports. When it was relocated to Six Flags St. Louis, the track was repainted green while the supports were repainted orange.

During its later years at Six Flags Over Texas, the train was ad-wrapped, advertising Charter Communications.
